script_text: Fact books are built with special parts to help you find stuff.
Once you know the parts, finding facts is fast!
The table of contents is up front.
It lists every chapter and what page it starts on.
Want chapter three? Look here first!
A heading is the big words at the top of a page.
It tells you what that page is about.
Labels are little words that point to parts of a picture.
Before you read, look at the headings and pictures. They tell you what is coming!
Now you are ready to find facts using book parts!
